title: 951. Flip Equivalent Binary Trees
question:
For a binary tree T, we can define a flip operation as follows: choose any node, and swap the left and right child subtrees.

A binary tree X is flip equivalent to a binary tree Y if and only if we can make X equal to Y after some number of flip operations.

Write a function that determines whether two binary trees are flip equivalent.  The trees are given by root nodes root1 and root2.

 

Example 1:

Input: root1 = [1,2,3,4,5,6,null,null,null,7,8], root2 = [1,3,2,null,6,4,5,null,null,null,null,8,7]
Output: true
Explanation: We flipped at nodes with values 1, 3, and 5.

 

Note:

  1. Each tree will have at most 100 nodes.
  2. Each value in each tree will be a unique integer in the range [0, 99].
code: # Definition for a binary tree node.\u000A# class TreeNode(object):\u000A#     def __init__(self, x):\u000A#         self.val \u003D x\u000A#         self.left \u003D None\u000A#         self.right \u003D None\u000A\u000Aclass Solution(object):\u000A    def flipEquiv(self, root1, root2):\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        :type root1: TreeNode\u000A        :type root2: TreeNode\u000A        :rtype: bool\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        \u000A        def _flipEquiv(l, r):\u000A            if not any([l, r]):\u000A                return True\u000A            elif not all([l, r]) or l.val !\u003D r.val:\u000A                return False\u000A            return _flipEquiv(l.left, r.right) and  _flipEquiv(l.right, r.left) or \u005C\u000A                 _flipEquiv(l.left, r.left) and  _flipEquiv(l.right, r.right)\u000A        return _flipEquiv(root1, root2)
